title: Resign and join RSS, Raj Thackeray slams feted IPS officer
content: If you feel affection for the Sangh, keep it in your heart, or if you want to speak about it publicly, then resign from service and join the Sangh or the BJP, says Maharashtra Navnirman Sena chief Raj Thackeray

title: Bhagyaraj passes away: T.N. to accord state honours, says CM Vijay | LIVE
content: He began his film career as an assistant director to veteran Tamil filmmaker Bharathiraja on the 1977 film 16 Vayathinile

title: Portrayal of pellet-hit victims in Bollywood film Chauhaan sparks anguish in Kashmir
content: A teaser for the Ajay Devgn-starrer Chauhaan shows a youth hit by pellets in the eyes during protests, with the actor describing it as ‘limited damage’ in a voiceover

title: Maharashtra Teacher Eligibility Test postponed day before exam after suspicion of paper leak
content: The decision to postpone the exam a day before the exam was taken after an investigation and search by the Bhiwandi Police in Thane district found people in possession of questions similar to those in the TET paper

title: Kolkata warehouse collapse: Six from a family in Bihar had come to work at Kolkata’s Taratala warehouse, only three return home
content: While three from the family from Munger had a miraculous escape and came out alive, three others had died in the Taratala warehouse collapse.

title: ‘Vijayam’: ‘Premam’ duo Nivin Pauly-Alphonse Puthren reunites
content: ‘Vijayam’ is the third collaboration of the celebrated actor-director duo after blockbusters ‘Neram’ ‘Premam’

title: Ram Temple Trust general secretary, member step down
content: Trust promises fair investigation into the donation embezzlement case in which eight have been arrested; it assures devotees that their offerings are safe, reiterates that the guilty will be punished

title: MDMK cuts ties with DMK alliance, pledges support to TVK government
content: MDMK General Secretary Vaiko announced that the party will extend its support to the Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam (TVK) government and will abstain from contesting the upcoming by-elections to back the ruling party.

title: Help end ‘proxy war’ by armed Kuki groups, Manipur Naga body urges PM
content: In a memorandum to the Prime Minister, Naga organisations say armed Kuki groups, including a Myanmar-based one, are waging a proxy war against the Nagas

title: Two BJP-led States question wage burden under VB-G RAM G
content: Bihar, MP and Jharkhand question 60:40 funding pattern; five seek wage revision, four raise concerns over non-working days; most flag pending dues

title: ED attaches property of retired Assam IPS officer in money laundering case
content: During the investigation, the ED officials found that Prasanta Kumar Dutta, promoted from the Assam Police Service, and his wife had net disproportionate assets worth ₹77.21 crore

title: Sufi saints and scholars learnt the ideals of co-existence from Indian scriptures: J&K L-G
content: Manoj Sinha also met with civil society groups, business leaders, and religious heads in Srinagar to discuss the upcoming Amarnath Yatra

title: Elevated corridor on NH5 likely if T.N. waives GST on construction materials
content: Meanwhile, a retired highways department official says that instead of an elevated corridor, it would be better to construct three flyovers

title: Pune fort murder | The scorpion’s sting
content: Ketan Agarwal was engaged to Siya Goyal in February and a lavish wedding was planned for November. On June 18, Ketan was found dead at the bottom of a 400-foot gorge at Lohagad Fort in Pune. What followed was a police investigation that peeled back, layer by layer, a plot allegedly months in the making

title: Inside Tiruvallur’s ammonia gas leak nightmare
content: The ammonia gas leak at a seafood processing unit in Tiruvallur district, near Chennai, has emerged as one of Tamil Nadu’s deadliest industrial disasters in recent years. This report traces what exactly happened, through eyewitness accounts, survivor testimonies, and medical evidence. The incident also raises serious questions about industrial safety, emergency preparedness, and the conditions of migrants employed far away from home

title: B. Manickam Tagore appointed as Tamil Nadu Congress Committee president
content: Mr. Tagore’s appointment comes at a time when the Congress has parted ways with its long-time ally, the DMK, and joined the TVK in a bid to secure a share in power in the State

title: Theft of youth’s future: Rahul Gandhi on Maharashtra TET ‘paper leak’
content: The LoP in Lok Sabha said the country's education and examination system has been turned into a “system of extortion, leaving every youngster in the country insecure"

title: Tamil Nadu cancels DFR tender for 12 ULBs, cites ‘confusion’ over waste management privatisation
content: To clear the confusion, the State has decided to review the existing system, identify gaps, bring further improvements where necessary, and take the decision in due course, an official said

title: PM Modi hails India-Seychelles ties as 'steady, strong and long-lasting'
content: Addressing the state dinner, President Herminie also affirmed the enduring bond between the two nations

title: Police flag over 30 safety issues lurking on city roads
content: PWD apprised of alleged faulty design of Chambakkara-Pettah road, while officials flag Container Road median issues, damaged approach roads to bridges, and risky potholes to NHAI

title: Amid row, revised low-alcoholic beverages tax rates included in Finance Bill
content: Rates pegged at 120% and 175% respectively for beverages with alcohol strength ranging from 0.5% to 10%, and those with strength above 10% and up to 20%. These were the rates announced by the Chief Minister in the Revised Budget

title: BJP MLC Ravi’s fact-finding report on cross-voting points to ‘precautions not taken’
content: A three-member committee led by C.T. Ravi, MLC, submitted its fact-finding report on cross-voting from the BJP in the recent Council polls to State chief B.Y. Vijayendra

title: Man arrested for allegedly cheating private schools across Tamil Nadu of over ₹100 crore
content: The police said the accused allegedly operated the unregistered organisation under the name of ‘Tamil Nadu Private Schools Association’ and claimed to have political and bureaucratic influence

title: Drama around Bidadi township escalates as Union Minister Kumaraswamy ‘waits’ for Chief Minister D.K. Shivakumar at Bairamangala for face-off
content: Kumaraswamy had invited Shivakumar for an ‘open discussion’ on the street of the Bairamangala, while the latter had invited the former to Vidhana Soudha for a discussion instead

title: Soumya Swaminathan honoured as Super Chennai's ‘Icon of the Month’
content: Dr. Swaminathan said growing up in the household of agricultural scientist M.S. Swaminathan and educationist Mina Swaminathan exposed her early to both science and social justice

title: Vellore Corporation to rejuvenate three irrigation tanks
content: Corporation officials said that as part of rejuvenation, water channels between hamlets in surrounding hills and lakes will be desilted and cleaned

title: Governor’s appointment of Sajitha Rani as Kerala Agricultural University V-C (in-charge) sparks row
content: Governor sets aside the name recommended by the government. Higher Education Minister says Arlekar acted unilaterally, calls decision a violation of democratic principles. Agriculture Minister says the government’s recommendation was made on the basis of ‘academic excellence,’ while Lok Bhavan considered ‘seniority’

title: Teachers may get compensatory off for Census duty on holidays
content: Teachers’ organisations seek duty leave. They also want teachers on daily wages to be posted to prevent academic disruption in schools.

title: Amid political turmoil, Congress opens door for KC(M) in K.M. Mani Hospital panel
content: Move brings to an end months of uncertainty over the reconstitution of the Hospital Management Committee of the K.M. Mani Memorial Government General Hospital

title: Tension as footwear is hurled at Congress MLA Pradeep Eshwar
content: Tension prevailed in Chickballapur town for sometime after a confrontation between Pradeep Eshwar, Congress MLA, and JD(S) workers erupted during a Kempe Gowda Jayanti programme, reaching a flashpoint as a slipper was allegedly hurled at the legislator during a procession

title: Lone station in ASR district receives 107.5 mm rain, other places A.P. receive light showers
content: Wet spell brings some relief to northern parts of State; thunderstorms, light rain and gusty winds are expected across the State till June 30

title: SRM-AP launches B. Tech in Health Engineering course
content: The new programme designed to integrate electronics, artificial intelligence (AI), computer science, and medical sciences to prepare students for careers in healthcare innovation and medical technology is scheduled to commence in August this year

title: "Wanted To Kill 15,000": Mumbai Man Caught With Toxic Pills At Muharram Event
content: At least 11 people had fallen ill after consuming the pills in Mumbai, said sources. They were rushed to the hospital and are now out of danger.

title: Siya Goyal Showed "Erratic Behaviour" Before Pune Fort Trip: Inside Details Of FIR
content: The FIR filed by Ketan Agarwal's father, Vishal Devichand Agarwal, on June 23 mentioned that Ketan recently told his family that Siya Goyal had been behaving erratically

title: Former Top Delhi Health Official Arrested In Multi-Crore Medical Scam Case
content: The case pertains to alleged large-scale financial irregularities in procurements worth several hundred crores of rupees by the Central Procurement Agency (CPA), functioning under the DGHS.

title: Switzerland Expert On How Noida Airport Used Climate-Friendly Cement
content: The Noida International Airport at Jewar has achieved a significant milestone by becoming India's first large scale civil engineering project to build with low carbon Limestone Calcined Clay Cement or LC3.

title: Long, Painful Wait By A Pile Of Rubble In Earthquake-Hit Venezuela
content: As the crews worked, Friday, it had been two days since that display of nature's power. One more day, and it is generally considered that a search for survivors becomes a search for bodies.

title: Chinese Dissident Who Fled To South Korea On Rubber Boat Reaches Canada
content: Dong Guangping, a 68-year-old former policeman, has been a thorn in Beijing's side for advocating political reform and human rights, and served multiple prison stints over the years.

title: Ex-Assam Top Cop's Rs 53 Crore Assets Attached In Corruption Case
content: In a major crackdown on alleged corruption, the Enforcement Directorate, has attached assets worth Rs 53 crore belonging to former Assam Police DIG Prashanta Kumar Dutta, who is facing charges in a disproportionate assets case

title: Woman Hides Drugs In Private Parts, Gets Caught With Six Others In Kerala
content: The anti-drug operation began during a vehicle inspection at Vaniyampara in Thrissur, where officers intercepted a Swift car travelling from a resort in Palakkad to Thrissur.

title: AAP Government Faces Heat Over Punjab's Power Crisis Ahead Of Assembly Polls
content: Farmers require a regular power supply to operate tubewells for irrigation, making electricity a lifeline for the state's agrarian economy.

title: Venezuela Earthquakes Live: Another Earthquake Of Magnitude 5.6 Hits Venezuela
content: Venezuela earthquake LIVE Updates: The second earthquake struck just a minute later, with a 7.5-magnitude, and its epicentre was 16 kilometres southwest of Moron.

title: Trump Warns Iran "Won't Exist" After Strikes, Gets "Experience Hell" Reply
content: The US had struck Iranian missile and drone storage sites and coastal radar positions on Friday, too, after it accused Tehran of attacking a cargo ship that was transiting the Strait of Hormuz.

title: Trump Taps Former Trooper Lance Schroyer To Lead Immigration Agency
content: Trump said on his Truth Social platform that his new pick for the immigration enforcement agency is a former US Marine and a "PATRIOT with real operational experience.

title: Hezbollah Slams Israel-Lebanon Deal, Questions Its Effectiveness
content: The agreement signed Friday in Washington links Israel's withdrawal from Lebanon to Hezbollah's disarmament, something the group rejects.

title: Three Arrested In Assam Over Alleged Hornbill Killing
content: The arrests were made on Friday following an investigation by Digboi Forest Division. The probe began after a disturbing video, reportedly filmed in the Digboi Forest Division in Tinsukia district, spread online

title: Police Seize Chetan Chaudhary's 2-Wheeler That He Used To Reach Pune Fort
content: According to police, the two-wheeler was allegedly used by the accused, Chetan, to travel from Pune to Lohagad Fort.

title: US-Iran War Live Updates: US-Iran Exchange Strikes, Deal Under Pressure
content: US-Iran War Live Updates: The clashes brought new tension to the negotiating process meant to end a war launched by the United States and Israel at the end of February.

title: Madhya Pradesh Forest Officer Suspended For Feeding Poha To Sambar Deer
content: Vinod Verma, In-charge Assistant Director, Itarsi, who was also serving as In-charge Superintendent of the Bori range in the Satpura Tiger Reserve, was placed under suspension with immediate effect.

title: 6 Terrorists Killed After Attack On Pak Army Base In Karachi, 4 Soldiers Dead
content: Pakistan has witnessed a surge in extremist attacks targeting police and security forces in recent years.

title: Bangladesh-China Joint Statement, Mongla Port's Implications For India Decoded
content: With Tarique Rahman's visit to China, experts in India believe some of the issues touched upon in the Bangladesh-China joint statement could impact India.

title: The Subtext Behind Congress's New UP Incharge On "Equal" Seat-Sharing
content: The Congress's newly appointed UP in-charge, Rajendra Pal Gautam Gautam, said those wishing to work on issues concerning the Bahujan community and strengthen the Constitution and democracy should unite

title: After mangoes, HOPCOMS plans to deliver fresh vegetables to your doorstep
content: HOPCOMS is currently studying the vegetable market and the kind of vegetables people prefer to consume across Bengaluru

title: Civilian dies in explosion near LoC in J&K
content: Officials identified the victim as Zabir Ahmad Bajad, son of Mohammad Rafiq Bajad, a resident of Galibal, Chandoosa in Baramulla district

title: APCC demands judicial probe into Ayodhya Ram temple donation case
content: Ayodhya Ram Janmabhoomi Teerth Kshetra Trust has betrayed the faith of millions through irregularities involving hundreds of crores of rupees, says Shivaji

title: AI should be considered as a tool to assist humans, says expert
content: Speaking at the inaugural session of a two-day workshop for faculty of P.B. Siddhartha Arts and Science College, NIEPA Head of the ICT and Project Management Unit, says teachers must take the lead in preparing students to meet the demands of a rapidly changing world

title: Naidu disburses ₹300 crore R&R funds to Veligonda project-displaced families
content: Thanks to the sacrifices made by the affected people, the project helps in providing irrigation water to 4.47 lakh acres and drinking water to 23 lakh people, and  transform the fortunes of four districts, says the Chief Minister

title: Centre debunks social media claims over Rajnath Singh’s Operation Sindoor speech
content: According to the clarification by the Ministry of Defence, Rajnath Singh’s remarks were specifically intended to counter a widely circulated false narrative at the time that claimed Indian Air Force pilots had been killed during Operation Sindoor

title: Elephant released into forest found dead
content: The elephant, which was radio-collared and released into the forest after straying into human habitation, was found lying motionless on Saturday

title: Jagan accuses TDP government of orchestrating attack on YSRCP leaders
content: YSRCP chief says party leaders had visited Amaravati at farmers’ invitation; alleges police coordinated the attack and farmers are being harassed in the name of the capital project

title: Versatile actor, director, screenwriter K. Bhagyaraj no more
content: Mentored by the late Bharathiraja, Bhagyaraj changed the perception of the Tamil film hero, proving that wit, intelligence, and ordinariness could be as compelling as physical prowess and a striking presence

title: 'Siya became Sonam's younger version': Raja Raghuvanshi's mother on Goyal's bail
content: The mother of a murdered Indore businessman has drawn striking parallels between her son's case and a recent alleged murder, accusing the accused of acting like a "younger version" of her arrested daughter-in-law. She expressed deep pain and urged for swift justice, criticizing the bail granted to her daughter-in-law, which she believes sends a dangerous message.

title: 'Was CM setting record of Ayodhya visits to oversee theft?' Akhilesh targets Yogi
content: Samajwadi Party chief Akhilesh Yadav has sharply criticized Uttar Pradesh Chief Minister Yogi Adityanath regarding the alleged Ram Temple donation embezzlement. Yadav questioned Adityanath's frequent visits to Ayodhya, suggesting they might have been to 'oversee ongoing thefts,' especially after an SIT report indicated tampered CCTV footage.

title: Lionel Messi creates history, becomes first player ever to ...
content: Lionel Messi etched his name in World Cup history, becoming the first player to score in seven consecutive matches with a stunning free-kick against Jordan.  This remarkable goal, his 19th in the tournament, extended his all-time scoring record.  The Argentine captain, rested for the start, came off the bench to secure a 3-1 victory, showcasing his enduring impact on the global stage.

title: Watch: Mamdani dives into pool with suits on, breaks dress code to kick off summer in NYC
content: New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani has revived a decade-old tradition by taking a ceremonial plunge into a public pool, marking the start of the summer swimming season. Dressed in a full suit and tie, Mamdani's dive into the Thomas Jefferson Pool in East Harlem also celebrated 90 years of public swimming in the city, a legacy initiated by Mayor Fiorello LaGuardia.

title: Jeff Bezos vs Zohran Mamdani debate: Kevin O’Leary says Amazon founder should run cities
content: Amazon founder Jeff Bezos sparked a debate on government efficiency and taxation, suggesting New York City's services would falter under his company's operational model. Billionaire Kevin O'Leary echoed these sentiments, calling the city a 'disaster' and warning against taxing the wealthy. Former Mayor Bill de Blasio criticized Bezos' remarks as out of touch. Meanwhile, Bezos also dismissed fears of AI causing job losses, predicting a labor shortage instead.

title: 'India needs to augment its strategic crude oil reserves': EY warns as import dependence crosses 90% in FY26
content: India's dependency on imported crude oil has exceeded 90%, creating a significant risk according to EY's findings. The consultancy emphasizes the urgent need for enhancing strategic oil reserves, which presently last just five days compared to China's extensive reserves. This is essential as domestic production wanes and fuel consumption rises, making India vulnerable to global supply disruptions and geopolitical tensions. A thorough reserve strategy is strongly advised.

title: NY Mayor Zohran Mamdani files motion to ban delivery app Motoclick: What lawsuit says
content: New York City is seeking to ban food delivery platform Motoclick for allegedly paying workers as little as $3.67 per hour, a fraction of the mandated $22.13 minimum wage. City officials filed a motion for a preliminary injunction, aiming to halt the company's operations unless it complies with labor laws.

title: From 2011 Fukushima nuclear disaster in Japan to GM at 14: The Ethan Vaz story
content: Born months after the Fukushima nuclear disaster forced his parents to leave Japan, Ethan Vaz's journey to becoming India's 96th Grandmaster at just 14 has been defined by sacrifice and remarkable family support. From replacing cartoons with chess to navigating financial uncertainty, Ethan's rise mirrors both extraordinary talent and a family's all-in pursuit of a dream.

title: Villagers carry pregnant woman on cot across river in MP, she delivers on way
content: A pregnant woman in Madhya Pradesh's Chhindwara district was carried on a cot across a river due to the absence of a bridge, delaying emergency medical care. Savita Vishwakarma delivered her baby mid-journey after an ambulance couldn't reach her remote village. This incident highlights the critical need for better infrastructure in the area, where residents have long demanded a bridge to ensure timely access to healthcare.

title: 'Rape is legal term, not medical': HC says torn hymen no ground to doubt survivor’s testimony
content: The Allahabad High Court upheld a 1983 rape conviction, emphasizing that a survivor's reliable testimony trumps medical findings about a hymen's condition. Justice Santosh Rai stated rape is a legal, not medical, term, and a torn hymen doesn't automatically grant an accused benefit of doubt. The court also corrected the original sentence, imposing a fine and directing the convicted man to surrender.

title: Michael Dell updates on Dell's Texas move days after Abbott's 'welcome home' message
content: Dell Technologies is officially moving its legal base from Delaware to Texas, with 97% of shareholders approving the change. CEO Michael Dell expressed pride in returning the company's legal home to the state where it was founded. This move aligns with Texas's growing appeal for major corporations, including Tesla, Coinbase, and Exxon Mobil, due to its favorable business climate and talent pool.

title: Sunita Ahuja on joining ‘Lock Upp: Sach Ya Sazaa’: ‘I said yes because of Ektaa Kapoor’
content: Sunita Ahuja, wife of actor Govinda, revealed her participation in 'Lock Upp: Sach Ya Sazaa' was influenced by producer Ektaa Kapoor and Netflix.  She expressed her commitment to honesty on the show, acknowledging the pressure but aiming to enjoy her experience. Meanwhile, Ektaa Kapoor explained that the revamped format of 'Lock Upp' necessitated changes, including new hosts Farah Khan and Riteish Deshmukh, and the absence of former host Kangana Ranaut.

title: Gaurav Khanna-Akanksha Chamola divorce: When Anupamaa actor spoke about not having kids
content: Television couple Gaurav Khanna and Akanksha Chamola are heading for divorce after a year of separation. Akanksha revealed the news on Lock Upp 2, stating the decision was mutual and amicable, stemming from differing life goals. Meanwhile, old clips of Gaurav discussing Akanksha's desire not to have children have resurfaced, adding another layer to their separation.

title: Buying agricultural land in India? Here's what you need to know
content: Purchasing agricultural land in India involves navigating diverse state-specific laws, often restricting buyers to agriculturists. The process demands meticulous verification of documents like title deeds and tax receipts, alongside registration and mutation. While NRIs generally cannot buy agricultural land directly, exceptions exist through inheritance, gifts, or rare RBI approvals. Buyers must confirm eligibility, land use, and clear titles, with legal counsel recommended for due diligence.

title: NFL trade rumors: Steelers, packers linked to $42m colts star
content: Jonathan Taylor's contract situation is sparking NFL trade rumors, with the Indianapolis Colts potentially moving the star running back if their season falters.  Teams like the Pittsburgh Steelers, Green Bay Packers, and Chicago Bears are reportedly interested.  A "Giannis-like" scenario is being discussed, where an elite player might be traded due to a team's uncertain future.  This situation highlights contract pressure and front office uncertainty for Indianapolis.

title: Pakistan: 4 troops, 6 terrorists killed in 90-minute gun battle at Karachi rangers HQ
content: Pakistani security forces thwarted a major militant assault on a Sindh Rangers compound in Karachi, killing six terrorists and apprehending one. The coordinated attack, claimed by Jamaat-ul-Ahrar, resulted in the tragic loss of four Rangers personnel. This incident marks the first significant terrorist strike in the city since October 2024, highlighting ongoing security challenges.

title: ‘Poison or push?’: Siya Goyal, her ‘lover’ Chetan searched online for 'ways to kill' Ketan
content: A chilling murder plot unfolded in Pune as a couple, Siya Goyal and Chetan Chaudhary, meticulously planned realtor Ketan Agrawal's death. Online searches for murder methods and rehearsed attempts at Lohagad Fort reveal the premeditated nature of the crime. Driven by social pressures and a desire to prevent an arranged marriage, the duo executed their plan, making the fatal push appear accidental.

title: Japan hit by 6.1-magnitude earthquake days after strong Iwate tremor, no tsunami threat
content: A magnitude 6.1 earthquake jolted Japan's Iwate Prefecture coast early Sunday, following a series of recent tremors. While no tsunami risk or immediate damage was reported, authorities are urging residents to remain vigilant due to the ongoing seismic activity. This latest quake occurred in an area previously hit by a stronger tremor, raising concerns about potential landslides as the typhoon season approaches.

title: 4 dead in Kentucky floods in US; emergency declared, rescue efforts intensify
content: The state of Kentucky is experiencing catastrophic flooding, leading to at least four confirmed fatalities, submerging residences, and significant infrastructure damage. Governor Andy Beshear has initiated a state of emergency while relentless rain threatens to escalate conditions further. As rescue teams mobilize, officials stress the importance of safety for residents facing this compounded disaster, marking yet another instance of severe weather's toll in the area.

title: Trainer aircraft propeller hits cadet pilot in Kanpur; girl hospitalised
content: A serious safety incident occurred at a Kanpur flying school when a girl cadet pilot was instructed to exit a twin-engine trainer aircraft with its propellers still spinning. The rotating blades struck her, causing severe injuries. The organization reportedly failed to notify aviation authorities about the accident. The cadet is currently hospitalized, and further details on the incident and official responses are pending.

title: Ladakh struggled with water for years, then Sonam Wangchuk created an ice stupa as a reservoir
content: In Ladakh's arid Himalayan landscape, a revolutionary solution to water scarcity has emerged. Engineer Sonam Wangchuk pioneered the 'ice stupa,' an artificial glacier built in a conical shape. These towers store winter meltwater, releasing it gradually during the crucial spring planting season. This low-cost, energy-free innovation ensures vital irrigation for agriculture, transforming a challenging environment and offering hope to communities worldwide facing similar water crises.

title: Hormuz got busy after months, now it's facing another roadblock
content: Ship traffic through the Strait of Hormuz surged briefly following US sanctions relief and a humanitarian evacuation effort. However, a fresh attack in the Gulf of Oman halted the operation, causing traffic to plummet. Iran's warning about safe passage routes further heightened concerns, leaving major global carriers hesitant to return to the volatile waterway.

title: Chinese naval activity continues around Taiwan after combat readiness drills
content: Taiwan's defence ministry reported six Chinese navy vessels operating near the island, continuing Beijing's regular military activity. This follows recent drills by Taiwan aimed at countering potential Chinese aggression. The island is also bolstering its defences, including its first domestically built submarine undergoing sea trials, amid escalating tensions and China's persistent claims over Taiwan.

title: Delhi 'feels' the heat at season-high 51.3°C; yellow rain alert for next two days
content: Delhi sweltered under a brutal heatwave, with the 'feel-like' temperature soaring to a season-high 51.3 degrees Celsius on Saturday. Despite the mercury hitting 41.3 degrees Celsius, high humidity made it feel much hotter. Nights offered little respite, remaining uncomfortably warm. Light rain and gusty winds are anticipated next week, but the monsoon's arrival is delayed, leaving the capital in a prolonged hot spell.

title: 'Meant to relieve pain': Rat poison capsules given at Moharram procession in Mumbai
content: A Pune man was apprehended in Byculla for distributing capsules containing zinc phosphide, a potent rat poison, to devotees during a Moharram procession. One devotee fell ill after consuming a capsule and required hospitalization. The accused claimed the capsules were for pain relief during mourning rituals. Police are investigating his motives, mental state, and how he obtained the large quantity of toxic substances.

title: 'Vigilant, lethal, and ready': US unleashes fresh strikes on 10 Iranian military targets — video
content: US forces launched strikes on 10 Iranian military targets near the Strait of Hormuz following an Iranian drone attack on a commercial oil tanker. The operation, ordered by President Trump, targeted surveillance, communication, and drone facilities. This escalation occurs despite an interim ceasefire, with Washington warning of further action if hostilities persist.

title: MEA cautions Kailash Mansarovar pilgrims against incomplete travel documents
content: The Ministry of External Affairs strongly advises Indian travelers undertaking the Kailash Mansarovar Yatra to obtain all required Chinese visas and entry permits prior to their departure. This message comes in light of recent incidents where 52 pilgrims found themselves stranded in Nepal due to inadequate paperwork. To avoid such issues, pilgrims should also confirm the legitimacy of their tour operator, especially now that the Nathu La route is accessible.

title: Ayodhya Ram temple 'donation theft': Probe points to wider embezzlement
content: The Ram temple donation embezzlement probe has intensified, now scrutinizing bank officials involved in handling cash deposits. Police are seeking custody of eight arrested individuals for in-depth interrogation to uncover the full extent of the alleged scam and recover more funds. Investigators are meticulously examining banking protocols and financial trails, suspecting potential collusion and evidence manipulation.

title: Desert shield, Indian spear: Why UAE is looking towards BrahMos and Akashteer
content: The UAE's interest in India's BrahMos missile and Akashteer air defence system highlights India's growing military tech prowess and West Asia's changing security landscape.  With threats like drones and missiles escalating, the UAE seeks a blend of offensive deterrence and defensive resilience. This combination offers a potent solution for modernizing their security architecture and maintaining strategic autonomy in a volatile region.

title: 'Will be forced to militarily complete the job': Trump warns Iran after 2nd ceasefire breach
content: The US launched fresh strikes on Iran after accusing Tehran of violating a ceasefire for a second consecutive day by attacking a commercial oil tanker near the Strait of Hormuz. This follows US retaliation for a drone attack on another vessel. Iran has warned of a "practical response" to the US actions, escalating regional tensions despite ongoing diplomatic efforts.

title: Shubman Gill meets football legend Ronaldinho at FIFA World Cup, photo goes viral
content: Indian cricket captain Shubman Gill recently shared a memorable encounter with football legend Ronaldinho at the FIFA World Cup 2026 in the United States. This meeting of sporting icons has captivated fans online. Gill's career is soaring, having recently reached No. 2 in the ICC ODI batting rankings. Meanwhile, Ronaldinho is making headlines again with a new signing in Italian football.

title: Erling Haaland and Isabel Haugseng Johansen combined net worth in 2026
content: Erling Haaland and Isabel Haugseng Johansen have built one of football's most talked-about partnerships. While Haaland's estimated $100 million net worth comes from his Manchester City contract, bonuses and endorsements, Johansen maintains a private lifestyle with an estimated net worth of $250,000-$300,000. The couple welcomed their first son in 2024 and continue to enjoy a family-focused life. Their combined wealth, luxury properties and growing global profile keep them among football's most prominent young couples.

title: Who was Andrea Bello? Héctor Bello's wife died shielding baby in earthquake
content: Andrea Bello lost her life while protecting her one-year-old daughter during Venezuela’s devastating earthquakes. Her husband, soccer player Héctor Bello, called her a hero and promised their daughter would always know what her mother did to save her. As rescue operations continue, Venezuela is mourning hundreds of lives lost while thousands of families wait for news of missing loved ones.

title: Justin Gaethje makes bold Ilia Topuria prediction after first career loss
content: Justin Gaethje believes Ilia Topuria’s first career loss will be mentally harder because of the unbeatable image he built before the fight. Speaking on The Jim Rome Show, Gaethje said confidence is important, but believing you cannot lose can make defeat much tougher to handle. His comments have divided MMA fans, with many debating whether Topuria will return stronger.

title: 'Welcome to the Jungle' BO: Akshay Kumar starrer storms past Rs 57 crore globally
content: 'Welcome to the Jungle', starring Akshay Kumar, has burst onto the silver screen, achieving a remarkable Rs 39 crore net debut in India over its opening weekend. Fans are embracing the film's playful nostalgia and ensemble talent, though reviews hint at some recycled humor reminiscent of earlier installments. Still, the sequel to the beloved 'Welcome' series has enjoyed a positive reception, captivating viewers in search of comedy.

title: June 30 deadline nears for pre-2025 waqf properties to get legal status
content: the cut-off for making corrections for properties whose details are al- ready uploaded ends on June 30 . all those who have still not uploaded information on their properties in keeping with the six months’ extension granted to them by various tribunals face the risk of missing the bus if they are unable to comply on time . around 5.87 lakh of the over 7.95 lakh waqf properties have been approved .

title: 7 India-bound cargo ships cross Hormuz in 3 days, 15 in queue
content: 44 ships have crossed the Strait of Hormuz in the past 72 hours . four are India-flagged and five are foreign-flagged . another 15 ships of Indian interest are in the Persian Gulf awaiting safe passage .

title: PM Modi govt’s silence on Gaza cost India morally, strategically: Sonia Gandhi
content: neta Sonia Gandhi said the silence on the genocide has cost India both in morality and strategic interest . she argued that India has alienated itself from its historical allies in Palestine, Iran and West Asia, as also global pubic opinion, and Pakistan has filled the space even though it is sponsor of terrorism .

title: Monsoon on the back foot: IMD warns of over 10% rainfall deficit
content: Vishwa Mohan is Senior Editor at The Times of India. He writes on environment, climate change, agriculture, water resources and clean energy, tracking policy issues and climate diplomacy. He has been covering Parliament since 2003 to see how politics shaped up domestic policy and India’s position at global platform. Before switching over to explore sustainable development issues, Vishwa had covered internal security and investigative agencies for more than a decade. Read More

title: Supreme Court takes 14 years on criminal case, 2 convicts die waiting
content: the apex court has taken more time than trial courts and HCs to decide a case . in one criminal case of culpable homicide not amounting to murder, SC took almost 14 years to decide the appeal . the appeal in SC came up for hearing on 12 dates over the last 14 years .

title: 'Never hid information on Op Sindoor deaths': Govt rejects misleading social media claims
content: defence ministry says information about the deaths of six military personnel was never hidden from the public . the ministry said in the press briefing held a day after operations were paused against Pakistan, the then director general of military operations had mentioned the loss of the lives of the armed forces personnel and civilians .

title: Passport row: Govt records reveal Indian passport holders abroad treated as citizens for using RTI
content: records of interministerial deliberations in 2010 over who can avail of right to information Act show that govt agreed that holders of Indian passports residing abroad can be treated as “citizens” for the purpose of availing of the benefits of information law . it differentiated the passport holders living abroad from Persons of Indian origin (PIOs) and Overseas Citizens of India (OCIs) saying that the latter two categories cannot take recourse to RTI .
